commands.review_summary:
The PR routes Codex app-server compaction through native thread compaction, limits context engines to post-native maintenance, adds doctor warnings/repair for stale Codex compaction overrides, updates Codex docs/changelog, and refreshes the SDK baseline hash.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high-confidence: current main calls an owning context engine's `compact()` before native Codex compaction even though the Codex runtime contract assigns compaction to Codex. I did not run a live OAuth session in this read-only review, but Conan-Scott's after-fix logs support the reported failure path.
